terms.definition: An isolated Python environment with its own interpreter link and site-packages, keeping a project's dependencies separate from others.
The standard-library module (python -m venv) that creates virtual environments.
The third-party predecessor to venv, offering faster creation and additional features.
Adjusting the current shell so python and pip resolve to a virtual environment's copies (e.g. source .venv/bin/activate).
The directory where installed third-party packages live; each virtual environment has its own.
